       .replace('/', '_')

    for col in X_logit.columns
]

# =========================================================
# ADD CONSTANT
# =========================================================

X_logit = sm.add_constant(
    X_logit,
    has_constant='add'
)

# =========================================================
# FIT LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# =========================================================

logit_model = sm.Logit(
    y,
    X_logit
)

result = logit_model.fit(
    method='lbfgs',
    maxiter=1000,
    disp=False
)

print(result.summary())

# =========================================================
# ODDS RATIOS
# =========================================================

params = result.params
conf = result.conf_int()

OR_table = pd.DataFrame({

    'Variable':
        params.index,

    'Adjusted_OR':
        np.exp(params),

    '95CI_Lower':
        np.exp(conf[0]),

    '95CI_Upper':
        np.exp(conf[1]),

    'P_value':
        result.pvalues
})

# =========================================================
# REMOVE INTERCEPT
# =========================================================

OR_table = OR_table[
    OR_table['Variable'] != 'const'
]

# =========================================================
# SIGNIFICANCE
# =========================================================

def significance_stars(p):

    if p < 0.001:
        return '***'

    elif p < 0.01:
        return '**'

    elif p < 0.05:
        return '*'

    else:
        return ''

OR_table['Significance'] = (
    OR_table['P_value']
    .apply(significance_stars)
)

OR_table['AOR (95% CI)'] = (

    OR_table['Adjusted_OR']
    .round(2)
    .astype(str)

    + ' ('

    + OR_table['95CI_Lower']
      .round(2)
      .astype(str)

    + '-'

    + OR_table['95CI_Upper']
      .round(2)
      .astype(str)

    + ')'
)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT RAW OR TABLE
# =========================================================

OR_table.to_csv(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'Logistic_OR_Table.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)

# =========================================================
# VARIABLE LABELS
# =========================================================

variable_labels = {

    'University_name':
        'Name of University',

    'Family_Type':
        'Family Type',

    'Internet_mode':
        'Internet Mode',

    'Average_DD_Use_Time':
        'Average Daily Digital Device Use Time',

    'Do_Multitask':
        'Do Multitask',

    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP':
        'Excessive Screen Time Affect Academic Performance',

    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD':
        'Feel Tired After Use of Digital Devices',

    'Experienced_ES_for_ST':
        'Experienced Eye Strain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST':
        'Experienced Headaches for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_NP_for_ST':
        'Experienced Neck Pain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_BP_for_ST':
        'Experienced Back Pain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_HD_for_ST':
        'Experienced Hand Discomfort for Screen Time',

    'Physical_activity_level':
        'Physical Activity Level',

    'Decline_Overall_Happyness':
        'Decline Overall Happiness',

    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity':
        'Difficulty in Offline Activity',

    'Impact_Learning_Ability':
        'Impact Learning Ability',

    'Overall_Health_Worsening':
        'Overall Health Worsening',

    'Sleep_amount_impact':
        'Sleep Amount Impact',

    'Varsity_hr_tiredness':
        'Varsity Hour Tiredness'
}

# =========================================================
# FUNCTION FOR MATCHING DUMMY NAMES
# =========================================================

def make_dummy_name(variable, category):

    return (
        f"{variable}_{category}"
        .replace('<', 'less_')
        .replace('>', 'greater_')
        .replace(',', '')
        .replace(' ', '_')
        .replace('-', '_')
        .replace('/', '_')
    )

# =========================================================
# BUILD PUBLICATION TABLE
# =========================================================

formatted_rows = []

for variable in display_order.keys():

    if variable not in reference_categories:
        continue

    reference = reference_categories[
        variable
    ]

    formatted_rows.append({

        'Variable':
            variable_labels.get(
                variable,
                variable
            ),

        'Category':
            reference,

        'AOR (95% CI)':
            'Reference',

        'P-value':
            '',

        'Significance':
            ''
    })

    for category in display_order[variable]:

        if category == reference:
            continue

        dummy_name = make_dummy_name(
            variable,
            category
        )

        matched = OR_table[
            OR_table['Variable']
            == dummy_name
        ]

        if matched.empty:
            continue

        row = matched.iloc[0]

        formatted_rows.append({

            'Variable': '',

            'Category':
                category,

            'AOR (95% CI)':
                row['AOR (95% CI)'],

            'P-value':
                (
                    '<0.001'
                    if row['P_value'] < 0.001
                    else f"{row['P_value']:.3f}"
                ),

            'Significance':
                row['Significance']
        })

# =========================================================
# CREATE FINAL TABLE
# =========================================================

publication_table = pd.DataFrame(
    formatted_rows
)

# =========================================================
# DISPLAY
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("PUBLICATION STYLE LOGISTIC TABLE")
print("===================================")

print(publication_table)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT
# =========================================================

publication_table.to_csv(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'Publication_Style_Logistic_Table.csv'
    ),

    index=False,
    encoding='utf-8-sig'
)

publication_table.to_excel(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'Publication_Style_Logistic_Table.xlsx'
    ),

    index=False
)

print(
    "\nPublication-style logistic regression table exported successfully."
)



















# =========================================================
# REBUILD X_logit FOR HIERARCHICAL ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

X_logit = model_df[significant_variables].copy()

# =========================================================
# STANDARDIZE CATEGORY LABELS
# =========================================================

# Physical Activity
X_logit['Physical_activity_level'] = (
    X_logit['Physical_activity_level']
    .replace({
        'Signifivantly decrease': 'Significantly decreased',
        'Somewhate decrease': 'Somewhat decreased'
    })
)

# Excessive Screen Time Affect Academic Performance
if 'Excessive_ST_affect_AP' in X_logit.columns:

    X_logit['Excessive_ST_affect_AP'] = (
        X_logit['Excessive_ST_affect_AP']
        .replace({
            'Yes,positively': 'Yes, positively',
            'Yes,negatively': 'Yes, negatively'
        })
    )

# Impact Learning Ability
if 'Impact_Learning_Ability' in X_logit.columns:

    X_logit['Impact_Learning_Ability'] = (
        X_logit['Impact_Learning_Ability']
        .replace({
            'Yes,positively': 'Yes, positively',
            'Yes,negatively': 'Yes, negatively'
        })
    )

# Remove extra spaces from all categorical variables
for col in X_logit.columns:

    if X_logit[col].dtype == 'object':

        X_logit[col] = (
            X_logit[col]
            .astype(str)
            .str.strip()
            .str.replace(r'\s+', ' ', regex=True)
        )

# =========================================================
# VERIFY BEFORE CATEGORICAL CONVERSION
# =========================================================

print("\nCHECKING CATEGORY COUNTS")

print(
    X_logit['Physical_activity_level']
    .value_counts(dropna=False)
)

print(
    X_logit['Excessive_ST_affect_AP']
    .value_counts(dropna=False)
)

if 'Impact_Learning_Ability' in X_logit.columns:

    print(
        X_logit['Impact_Learning_Ability']
        .value_counts(dropna=False)
    )
# =========================================================
# DISPLAY ORDER
# =========================================================

display_order = {

    'University_name': [
        'Islamic University',
        'HSTU',
        'University of Dhaka',
        'SUST',
        'Cumilla University',
        'PUST',
        'JUST'
    ],

    'Family_Type': [
        'Single Family',
        'Joint Family'
    ],

    'Internet_mode': [
        'Mobile data connection',
        'Broadband connection'
    ],

    'Average_DD_Use_Time': [
        '<2',
        '2-4',
        '4-6',
        '>6'
    ],

    'Do_Multitask': [
        'Rarely',
        'Often',
        'Sometimes',
        'Always'
    ],

    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD': [
        'Rarely',
        'Sometimes',
        'Often',
        'Always'
    ],

    'Experienced_ES_for_ST': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ],

    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ],

    'Experienced_NP_for_ST': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ],

    'Experienced_BP_for_ST': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ],

    'Experienced_HD_for_ST': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ],

    'Physical_activity_level': [
        'Significantly decreased',
        'Somewhat decreased',
        'Remained the same'
    ],

    'Sleep_amount_impact': [
        'Yes',
        'No',
        'Not sure'
    ],

    'Varsity_hr_tiredness': [
        'Never',
        'Sometimes',
        'Often',
        'Always'
    ],

    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP': [
        'Yes, negatively',
        'Yes, positively',
        'No impact'
    ],

    'Decline_Overall_Happyness': [
        'Yes, significantly',
        'Yes, somewhat',
        'No noticeable change'
    ],

    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity': [
        'Yes, frequently',
        'Yes, occasionally',
        'No'
    ],

    'Impact_Learning_Ability': [
        'Yes, negatively',
        'Yes, positively',
        'No impact'
    ],

    'Overall_Health_Worsening': [
        'No',
        'Yes'
    ]
}

# =========================================================
# REFERENCE CATEGORIES
# =========================================================

reference_categories_model = {

    'University_name': 'University of Dhaka',
    'Family_Type': 'Joint Family',
    'Internet_mode': 'Broadband connection',
    'Average_DD_Use_Time': '<2',
    'Do_Multitask': 'Rarely',
    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD': 'Rarely',
    'Experienced_ES_for_ST': 'No',
    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST': 'No',
    'Experienced_NP_for_ST': 'No',
    'Experienced_BP_for_ST': 'No',
    'Experienced_HD_for_ST': 'No',
    'Physical_activity_level': 'Remained the same',
    'Sleep_amount_impact': 'No',
    'Varsity_hr_tiredness': 'Never',
    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP': 'No impact',
    'Decline_Overall_Happyness': 'No noticeable change',
    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity': 'No',
    'Impact_Learning_Ability': 'No impact',
    'Overall_Health_Worsening': 'No'
}

# =========================================================
# APPLY REFERENCE CATEGORIES
# =========================================================

for variable, reference in reference_categories_model.items():

    if variable not in X_logit.columns:
        continue

    ordered_categories = (
        [reference]
        +
        [
            x
            for x in display_order[variable]
            if x != reference
        ]
    )

    X_logit[variable] = pd.Categorical(
        X_logit[variable],
        categories=ordered_categories,
        ordered=True
    )

# =========================================================
# CREATE DUMMIES
# =========================================================

X_logit = pd.get_dummies(
    X_logit,
    drop_first=True,
    dtype=float
)

# =========================================================
# CLEAN COLUMN NAMES
# =========================================================

X_logit.columns = [

    col.replace('<', 'less_')
       .replace('>', 'greater_')
       .replace(',', '')
       .replace(' ', '_')
       .replace('-', '_')
       .replace('/', '_')

    for col in X_logit.columns
]

# =========================================================
# VERIFY PHYSICAL ACTIVITY DUMMIES
# =========================================================

print("\nPHYSICAL ACTIVITY DUMMIES")

print(
    X_logit[
        [
            'Physical_activity_level_Significantly_decreased',
            'Physical_activity_level_Somewhat_decreased'
        ]
    ].sum()
)


















# =========================================================
# HIERARCHICAL / BLOCK-WISE LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# CMD STUDY
# DISTAL → PROXIMAL FRAMEWORK
# =========================================================

print("\nRUNNING HIERARCHICAL LOGISTIC REGRESSION")

# =========================================================
# DEPENDENT VARIABLE
# =========================================================

y_hier = y.copy()

# =========================================================
# HIERARCHICAL MODEL BLOCKS
# =========================================================

hierarchical_models = {

    'Model_I_Sociodemographic': [

        'University_name',
        'Family_Type'
    ],

    'Model_II_Digital_Behaviour': [

        'University_name',
        'Family_Type',

        'Internet_mode',
        'Average_DD_Use_Time',
        'Do_Multitask'
    ],

    'Model_III_Physical_Health': [

        'University_name',
        'Family_Type',

        'Internet_mode',
        'Average_DD_Use_Time',
        'Do_Multitask',

        'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD',

        'Experienced_ES_for_ST',
        'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST',
        'Experienced_NP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_BP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_HD_for_ST',

        'Physical_activity_level',

        'Sleep_amount_impact',

        'Varsity_hr_tiredness'
    ],

    'Model_IV_Full_Adjusted': [

        'University_name',
        'Family_Type',

        'Internet_mode',
        'Average_DD_Use_Time',
        'Do_Multitask',

        'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD',

        'Experienced_ES_for_ST',
        'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST',
        'Experienced_NP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_BP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_HD_for_ST',

        'Physical_activity_level',

        'Sleep_amount_impact',

        'Varsity_hr_tiredness',

        'Excessive_ST_affect_AP',

        'Decline_Overall_Happyness',

        'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity',

        'Impact_Learning_Ability',

        'Overall_Health_Worsening'
    ]
}

# =========================================================
# STORAGE OBJECTS
# =========================================================

all_model_results = []
model_fit_summary = []

# =========================================================
# NULL MODEL (INTERCEPT ONLY)
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("NULL MODEL")
print("===================================")

X_null = pd.DataFrame({
    'const': np.ones(len(y_hier))
})

null_model = sm.Logit(
    y_hier,
    X_null
)

null_result = null_model.fit(
    method='lbfgs',
    maxiter=1000,
    disp=False
)

model_fit_summary.append({

    "Model":
        "Null_Model",

    "Pseudo_R2":
        0.000,

    "AIC":
        round(null_result.aic, 2),

    "BIC":
        round(null_result.bic, 2),

    "LogLikelihood":
        round(null_result.llf, 2),

    "LR_Improvement":
        np.nan,

    "LR_Pvalue":
        np.nan
})

# =========================================================
# USE NULL MODEL AS BASELINE
# =========================================================

previous_ll = null_result.llf
previous_nparam = len(null_result.params)

# =========================================================
# RUN HIERARCHICAL MODELS
# =========================================================

for model_name, variable_list in hierarchical_models.items():

    print("\n===================================")
    print(model_name)
    print("===================================")

    available_variables = []

    for variable in variable_list:

        matched_columns = [

            col
            for col in X_logit.columns
            if col.startswith(variable + "_")
        ]

        available_variables.extend(
            matched_columns
        )

    available_variables = list(
        dict.fromkeys(available_variables)
    )

    X_model = X_logit[
        available_variables
    ].copy()

    # ---------------------------------------------
    # REMOVE CONSTANT COLUMNS
    # ---------------------------------------------

    X_model = X_model.loc[
        :,
        X_model.nunique() > 1
    ]

    # ---------------------------------------------
    # ADD CONSTANT
    # ---------------------------------------------

    X_model = add_constant(
        X_model,
        has_constant="add"
    )

    X_model = X_model.astype(float)

    try:

        logit_model = sm.Logit(
            y_hier,
            X_model
        )

        result = logit_model.fit(
            method='lbfgs',
            maxiter=1000,
            disp=False
        )

        # =========================================
        # LR TEST VS PREVIOUS MODEL
        # =========================================

        lr_stat = 2 * (
            result.llf -
            previous_ll
        )

        df_diff = (
            len(result.params)
            -
            previous_nparam
        )

        lr_p = chi2.sf(
            lr_stat,
            df_diff
        )

        # =========================================
        # SAVE MODEL FIT
        # =========================================

        model_fit_summary.append({

            "Model":
                model_name,

            "Pseudo_R2":
                round(
                    result.prsquared,
                    3
                ),

            "AIC":
                round(
                    result.aic,
                    2
                ),

            "BIC":
                round(
                    result.bic,
                    2
                ),

            "LogLikelihood":
                round(
                    result.llf,
                    2
                ),

            "LR_Improvement":
                round(
                    lr_stat,
                    3
                ),

            "LR_Pvalue":
                round(
                    lr_p,
                    5
                )
        })

        # =========================================
        # SAVE CURRENT MODEL AS PREVIOUS
        # =========================================

        previous_ll = result.llf

        previous_nparam = len(
            result.params
        )

        # =========================================
        # OR TABLE
        # =========================================

        params = result.params

        conf = result.conf_int()

        conf.columns = [
            "Lower_CI",
            "Upper_CI"
        ]

        temp_df = pd.DataFrame({

            "Model":
                model_name,

            "Variable":
                params.index,

            "Adjusted_OR":
                np.exp(params),

            "95CI_Lower":
                np.exp(
                    conf["Lower_CI"]
                ),

            "95CI_Upper":
                np.exp(
                    conf["Upper_CI"]
                ),

            "P_value":
                result.pvalues
        })

        temp_df = temp_df[
            temp_df["Variable"] != "const"
        ]

        temp_df["AOR (95% CI)"] = (

            temp_df["Adjusted_OR"]
            .round(2)
            .astype(str)

            + " ("

            + temp_df["95CI_Lower"]
              .round(2)
              .astype(str)

            + "-"

            + temp_df["95CI_Upper"]
              .round(2)
              .astype(str)

            + ")"
        )

        temp_df["Significance"] = np.where(

            temp_df["P_value"] < 0.001,
            "***",

            np.where(
                temp_df["P_value"] < 0.01,
                "**",

                np.where(
                    temp_df["P_value"] < 0.05,
                    "*",
                    ""
                )
            )
        )

        all_model_results.append(
            temp_df
        )

        print(
            f"\nPseudo R²: {result.prsquared:.3f}"
        )

        print(
            f"AIC: {result.aic:.2f}"
        )

        print(
            f"BIC: {result.bic:.2f}"
        )

        print(
            f"LR χ² Improvement: {lr_stat:.3f}"
        )

        print(
            f"LR p-value: {lr_p:.5f}"
        )

    except Exception as e:

        print(
            f"\nERROR IN {model_name}"
        )

        print(e)

# =========================================================
# COMBINE RESULTS
# =========================================================

hierarchical_df = pd.concat(
    all_model_results,
    ignore_index=True
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL FIT TABLE
# =========================================================

model_fit_df = pd.DataFrame(
    model_fit_summary
)

print("\n===================================")
print("MODEL FIT COMPARISON")
print("===================================")

print(model_fit_df)


# =========================================================
# VARIABLE LABELS
# =========================================================

variable_labels = {

    'University_name':
        'Name of University',

    'Family_Type':
        'Family Type',

    'Internet_mode':
        'Internet Mode',

    'Average_DD_Use_Time':
        'Average Daily Digital Device Use Time',

    'Do_Multitask':
        'Do Multitask',

    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP':
        'Excessive Screen Time Affect Academic Performance',

    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD':
        'Feel Tired After Use of Digital Devices',

    'Experienced_ES_for_ST':
        'Experienced Eye Strain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST':
        'Experienced Headaches for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_NP_for_ST':
        'Experienced Neck Pain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_BP_for_ST':
        'Experienced Back Pain for Screen Time',

    'Experienced_HD_for_ST':
        'Experienced Hand Discomfort for Screen Time',

    'Physical_activity_level':
        'Physical Activity Level',

    'Decline_Overall_Happyness':
        'Decline Overall Happiness',

    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity':
        'Difficulty in Offline Activity',

    'Impact_Learning_Ability':
        'Impact Learning Ability',

    'Overall_Health_Worsening':
        'Overall Health Worsening',

    'Sleep_amount_impact':
        'Sleep Amount Impact',

    'Varsity_hr_tiredness':
        'Varsity Hour Tiredness'
}

# =========================================================
# REFERENCE CATEGORIES
# =========================================================

reference_categories = {

    'University_name':
        'University of Dhaka',

    'Family_Type':
        'Joint Family',

    'Internet_mode':
        'Broadband connection',

    'Average_DD_Use_Time':
        '<2',

    'Do_Multitask':
        'Rarely',

    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP':
        'No impact',

    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD':
        'Rarely',

    'Experienced_ES_for_ST':
        'No',

    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST':
        'No',

    'Experienced_NP_for_ST':
        'No',

    'Experienced_BP_for_ST':
        'No',

    'Experienced_HD_for_ST':
        'No',

    'Physical_activity_level':
        'Remained the same',

    'Decline_Overall_Happyness':
        'No noticeable change',

    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity':
        'No',

    'Impact_Learning_Ability':
        'No impact',

    'Overall_Health_Worsening':
        'No',

    'Sleep_amount_impact':
        'No',

    'Varsity_hr_tiredness':
        'Never'
}

# =========================================================
# CREATE PUBLICATION STYLE HIERARCHICAL TABLE
# =========================================================

publication_rows = []

for model in hierarchical_df['Model'].unique():

    model_subset = hierarchical_df[
        hierarchical_df['Model'] == model
    ]

    for variable in reference_categories.keys():

        matching_rows = model_subset[
            model_subset['Variable'].str.startswith(
                variable + "_"
            )
        ]

        if matching_rows.empty:
            continue

        # -------------------------------------------------
        # REFERENCE CATEGORY
        # -------------------------------------------------

        publication_rows.append({

            'Model':
                model,

            'Variable':
                variable_labels[variable],

            'Category':
                reference_categories[variable],

            'AOR (95% CI)':
                'Reference',

            'P-value':
                '',

            'Significance':
                ''
        })

        # -------------------------------------------------
        # NON-REFERENCE CATEGORIES
        # -------------------------------------------------

        for _, row in matching_rows.iterrows():

            category = row['Variable'].replace(
                variable + "_",
                ""
            )

            category = (
                category
                .replace("_", " ")
                .replace("greater", ">")
                .replace("less", "<")
            )

            aor_ci = (

                f"{row['Adjusted_OR']:.2f} "
                f"({row['95CI_Lower']:.2f}-"
                f"{row['95CI_Upper']:.2f})"
            )

            if row['P_value'] < 0.001:

                p_text = "<0.001"

            else:

                p_text = f"{row['P_value']:.3f}"

            publication_rows.append({

                'Model':
                    model,

                'Variable':
                    '',

                'Category':
                    category,

                'AOR (95% CI)':
                    aor_ci,

                'P-value':
                    p_text,

                'Significance':
                    row['Significance']
            })

# =========================================================
# FINAL TABLE
# =========================================================

hierarchical_publication_table = pd.DataFrame(
    publication_rows
)

print("\n===================================")
print("PUBLICATION STYLE HIERARCHICAL TABLE")
print("===================================")

print(hierarchical_publication_table)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT FILES
# =========================================================

model_fit_df.to_csv(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Model_Fit_Comparison.csv"
    ),
    index=False
)

model_fit_df.to_excel(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Model_Fit_Comparison.xlsx"
    ),
    index=False
)

hierarchical_df.to_csv(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Logistic_Regression.csv"
    ),
    index=False
)

hierarchical_df.to_excel(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Logistic_Regression.xlsx"
    ),
    index=False
)

hierarchical_publication_table.to_csv(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Publication_Table.csv"
    ),
    index=False,
    encoding="utf-8-sig"
)

hierarchical_publication_table.to_excel(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Hierarchical_Publication_Table.xlsx"
    ),
    index=False
)

print(
    "\nHierarchical logistic regression exported successfully."
)

print(
    "\nPublication-style hierarchical table exported successfully."
)












# =========================================================
# INDIVIDUAL PREDICTOR DOMINANCE ANALYSIS
# CMD STUDY
# =========================================================

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance

print("\nRUNNING INDIVIDUAL PREDICTOR DOMINANCE ANALYSIS")

# =========================================================
# VARIABLES
# =========================================================

dominance_predictors = [

    'University_name',
    'Family_Type',
    'Internet_mode',
    'Average_DD_Use_Time',
    'Do_Multitask',
    'Excessive_ST_affect_AP',
    'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD',
    'Experienced_ES_for_ST',
    'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST',
    'Experienced_NP_for_ST',
    'Experienced_BP_for_ST',
    'Experienced_HD_for_ST',
    'Physical_activity_level',
    'Decline_Overall_Happyness',
    'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity',
    'Impact_Learning_Ability',
    'Overall_Health_Worsening',
    'Sleep_amount_impact',
    'Varsity_hr_tiredness'
]

# =========================================================
# DATA
# =========================================================

dom_df = model_df.copy()

X_dom = dom_df[
    dominance_predictors
].copy()

y_dom = dom_df[
    'CMD_binary'
]

# =========================================================
# DUMMIES
# =========================================================

X_dom = pd.get_dummies(
    X_dom,
    drop_first=True
)

# =========================================================
# MODEL
# =========================================================

dom_model = LogisticRegression(
    max_iter=5000,
    random_state=123
)

dom_model.fit(
    X_dom,
    y_dom
)

# =========================================================
# PERMUTATION IMPORTANCE
# =========================================================

perm = permutation_importance(

    dom_model,

    X_dom,

    y_dom,

    n_repeats=100,

    random_state=123
)

dominance_results = pd.DataFrame({

    'Predictor':
        X_dom.columns,

    'Importance':
        perm.importances_mean
})

dominance_results = dominance_results.sort_values(

    'Importance',

    ascending=False
)

dominance_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
] = (

    dominance_results['Importance']

    /

    dominance_results['Importance'].sum()

) * 100

dominance_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
] = dominance_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
].round(2)

print("\n===================================")
print("INDIVIDUAL PREDICTOR DOMINANCE")
print("===================================")

print(dominance_results)

dominance_results.to_csv(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Individual_Dominance_Analysis.csv"
    ),

    index=False
)

dominance_results.to_excel(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Individual_Dominance_Analysis.xlsx"
    ),

    index=False
)





# =========================================================
# DOMAIN LEVEL DOMINANCE ANALYSIS
# CMD STUDY
# =========================================================

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score

print("\nRUNNING DOMAIN LEVEL DOMINANCE ANALYSIS")

domain_results = []

# =========================================================
# DOMAINS
# =========================================================

domains = {

    'Sociodemographic': [

        'University_name',
        'Family_Type'
    ],

    'Digital_Behaviour': [

        'Internet_mode',
        'Average_DD_Use_Time',
        'Do_Multitask'
    ],

    'Physical_Health': [

        'Feel_tired_after_Use_DD',

        'Experienced_ES_for_ST',
        'Experienced_Headaches_for_ST',
        'Experienced_NP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_BP_for_ST',
        'Experienced_HD_for_ST',

        'Physical_activity_level',

        'Sleep_amount_impact',

        'Varsity_hr_tiredness'
    ],

    'Psychosocial_Academic': [

        'Excessive_ST_affect_AP',

        'Decline_Overall_Happyness',

        'Difficulty_in_Offline_Activity',

        'Impact_Learning_Ability',

        'Overall_Health_Worsening'
    ]
}

# =========================================================
# FULL MODEL
# =========================================================

all_vars = []

for v in domains.values():

    all_vars.extend(v)

X_full = model_df[
    all_vars
].copy()

X_full = pd.get_dummies(
    X_full,
    drop_first=True
)

y_full = model_df[
    'CMD_binary'
]

full_model = LogisticRegression(
    max_iter=5000,
    random_state=123
)

full_model.fit(
    X_full,
    y_full
)

full_auc = roc_auc_score(

    y_full,

    full_model.predict_proba(
        X_full
    )[:,1]
)

# =========================================================
# REMOVE EACH DOMAIN
# =========================================================

for domain_name, remove_vars in domains.items():

    remaining_vars = [

        v for v in all_vars

        if v not in remove_vars
    ]

    X_temp = model_df[
        remaining_vars
    ].copy()

    X_temp = pd.get_dummies(
        X_temp,
        drop_first=True
    )

    temp_model = LogisticRegression(
        max_iter=5000,
        random_state=123
    )

    temp_model.fit(
        X_temp,
        y_full
    )

    temp_auc = roc_auc_score(

        y_full,

        temp_model.predict_proba(
            X_temp
        )[:,1]
    )

    contribution = (
        full_auc -
        temp_auc
    )

    domain_results.append({

        'Domain':
            domain_name,

        'AUC_Reduction':
            contribution
    })

# =========================================================
# RESULTS
# =========================================================

domain_results = pd.DataFrame(
    domain_results
)

domain_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
] = (

    domain_results[
        'AUC_Reduction'
    ]

    /

    domain_results[
        'AUC_Reduction'
    ].sum()

) * 100

domain_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
] = domain_results[
    'Relative_Contribution_%'
].round(2)

domain_results = domain_results.sort_values(

    'Relative_Contribution_%',

    ascending=False
)

print("\n===================================")
print("DOMAIN LEVEL DOMINANCE")
print("===================================")

print(domain_results)


# =========================================================
# FIGURE: DOMAIN LEVEL DOMINANCE ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plot_df = domain_results.copy()

# Different colors for each domain

colors = [

    '#1f77b4',   # Digital Behaviour (blue)

    '#ff7f0e',   # Sociodemographic (orange)

    '#2ca02c',   # Psychosocial Academic (green)

    '#d62728'    # Physical Health (red)
]

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

bars = plt.bar(

    plot_df['Domain'],

    plot_df['Relative_Contribution_%'],

    color=colors
)

plt.ylabel(
    'Relative Contribution (%)',
    fontsize=12
)

plt.xlabel(
    'Predictor Domain',
    fontsize=12
)

plt.title(
    'Relative Contribution of Predictor Domains to CMD',
    fontsize=13,
    fontweight='bold'
)

# =========================================================
# ADD VALUE LABELS
# =========================================================

for bar in bars:

    height = bar.get_height()

    plt.text(

        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,

        height + 0.5,

        f'{height:.2f}%',

        ha='center',

        fontsize=10,

        fontweight='bold'
    )

# =========================================================
# CLEAN DOMAIN LABELS
# =========================================================

plt.xticks(

    ticks=range(len(plot_df)),

    labels=[

        'Digital\nBehaviour',

        'Sociodemographic',

        'Psychosocial\nAcademic',

        'Physical\nHealth'
    ]
)

plt.ylim(

    0,

    max(
        plot_df['Relative_Contribution_%']
    ) + 10
)

plt.tight_layout()

# =========================================================
# SAVE FIGURE
# =========================================================

plt.savefig(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Domain_Dominance_CMD.png'
    ),

    dpi=600,

    bbox_inches='tight'
)

plt.show()

print(
    "\nDomain Dominance Analysis exported successfully."
)


domain_results.to_csv(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Domain_Dominance_Analysis.csv"
    ),

    index=False
)

domain_results.to_excel(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        "Domain_Dominance_Analysis.xlsx"
    ),

    index=False
)





















# =========================================================
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# SCREEN TIME → SLEEP IMPACT → CMD
# =========================================================

import pingouin as pg
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os

print("\nRUNNING MEDIATION ANALYSIS")

# =========================================================
# CREATE DATASET
# =========================================================

med_df = model_df.copy()

# =========================================================
# SCREEN TIME SCORE
# =========================================================

med_df['DD_Time_Score'] = med_df[
    'Average_DD_Use_Time'
].map({

    '<2': 1,
    '2-4': 2,
    '4-6': 3,
    '>6': 4
})

# =========================================================
# SLEEP IMPACT SCORE
# Actual Categories:
# No / Not sure / Yes
# =========================================================

med_df['Sleep_Score'] = med_df[
    'Sleep_amount_impact'
].map({

    'No': 0,
    'Not sure': 1,
    'Yes': 2
})

# =========================================================
# KEEP NUMERIC
# =========================================================

med_df['DD_Time_Score'] = pd.to_numeric(
    med_df['DD_Time_Score'],
    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['Sleep_Score'] = pd.to_numeric(
    med_df['Sleep_Score'],
    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['CMD_binary'] = pd.to_numeric(
    med_df['CMD_binary'],
    errors='coerce'
)

# =========================================================
# REMOVE MISSING
# =========================================================

med_df_sleep = med_df.dropna(
    subset=[
        'DD_Time_Score',
        'Sleep_Score',
        'CMD_binary'
    ]
)

print(
    f"\nSample size for sleep mediation = "
    f"{len(med_df_sleep)}"
)

# =========================================================
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

med_sleep = pg.mediation_analysis(

    data=med_df_sleep,

    x='DD_Time_Score',

    m='Sleep_Score',

    y='CMD_binary',

    n_boot=5000,

    seed=123
)

print("\n===================================")
print("MEDIATION ANALYSIS")
print("SCREEN TIME → SLEEP IMPACT → CMD")
print("===================================")

print(med_sleep)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT
# =========================================================

med_sleep.to_excel(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'Mediation_SleepImpact_CMD.xlsx'
    ),

    index=False
)

med_sleep.to_csv(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'Mediation_SleepImpact_CMD.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)

print(
    "\nSleep mediation analysis exported successfully."
)

# =========================================================
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# SCREEN TIME → LEARNING IMPACT → CMD
# =========================================================

print("\nRUNNING LEARNING IMPACT MEDIATION ANALYSIS")

# =========================================================
# CREATE LEARNING SCORE
# =========================================================

med_df['Learning_Score'] = med_df[
    'Impact_Learning_Ability'
].map({

    'No impact': 0,

    'Yes, positively': 1,

    'Yes, negatively': 2
})

# =========================================================
# NUMERIC CONVERSION
# =========================================================

med_df['Learning_Score'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['Learning_Score'],

    errors='coerce'
)

# =========================================================
# CHECK MISSING VALUES
# =========================================================

print("\nMissing values:")

print(
    med_df[
        [
            'DD_Time_Score',
            'Learning_Score',
            'CMD_binary'
        ]
    ].isnull().sum()
)

# =========================================================
# REMOVE MISSING VALUES
# =========================================================

med_df_learning = med_df.dropna(

    subset=[

        'DD_Time_Score',

        'Learning_Score',

        'CMD_binary'
    ]
)

print(
    f"\nSample size for learning mediation = "
    f"{len(med_df_learning)}"
)

# =========================================================
# CHECK DISTRIBUTION
# =========================================================

print("\nLearning Score Distribution")

print(
    med_df_learning[
        'Learning_Score'
    ].value_counts()
)

# =========================================================
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

med_learning = pg.mediation_analysis(

    data=med_df_learning,

    x='DD_Time_Score',

    m='Learning_Score',

    y='CMD_binary',

    n_boot=5000,

    seed=123
)

# =========================================================
# DISPLAY RESULTS
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("MEDIATION ANALYSIS")
print("SCREEN TIME → LEARNING IMPACT → CMD")
print("===================================")

print(med_learning)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT EXCEL
# =========================================================

med_learning.to_excel(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Mediation_LearningImpact_CMD.xlsx'
    ),

    index=False
)

# =========================================================
# EXPORT CSV
# =========================================================

med_learning.to_csv(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Mediation_LearningImpact_CMD.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)

print(
    "\nLearning mediation analysis exported successfully."
)
















'''##=====================New=====================##
# =========================================================
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE → CMD
# PUBLICATION-QUALITY APPROACH
# =========================================================

import statsmodels.api as sm

print("\nRUNNING MEDIATION ANALYSIS")


# =========================================================
# CREATE DATASET
# =========================================================

med_df = df[[

    'Average_DD_Use_Time',

    'Sleep_Pattern_Change',

    'CMD'
]].copy()


# =========================================================
# REMOVE MISSING VALUES
# =========================================================

med_df = med_df.dropna()


# =========================================================
# CONVERT SCREEN TIME TO NUMERIC
# =========================================================

screen_mapping = {

    '<2': 1,

    '2-4': 2,

    '4-6': 3,

    '>6': 4
}

med_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = med_df[
    
    'Average_DD_Use_Time'
].map(screen_mapping)


# =========================================================
# CONVERT SLEEP CHANGE TO NUMERIC
# =========================================================
# CHECK UNIQUE VALUES
# =========================================================

print("\nSLEEP PATTERN UNIQUE VALUES")

print(

    med_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change']
    .value_counts(dropna=False)
)


# =========================================================
# CLEAN STRING
# =========================================================

med_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change'] = (

    med_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change']
    .astype(str)
    .str.strip()
    .str.lower()
)


# =========================================================
# CONVERT TO NUMERIC
# =========================================================

med_df['Sleep_Num'] = np.where(

    med_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change'].isin([

        'no',
        'no change',
        'normal'
    ]),

    0,

    1
)


# =========================================================
# CONVERT CMD TO NUMERIC
# =========================================================

med_df['CMD_Num'] = np.where(

    med_df['CMD'] == 'CMD Present',

    1,

    0
)


# =========================================================
# FORCE NUMERIC TYPES
# =========================================================

med_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['ScreenTime_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['Sleep_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['Sleep_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['CMD_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['CMD_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)


# =========================================================
# REMOVE ANY REMAINING MISSING VALUES
# =========================================================

med_df = med_df.dropna()


# =========================================================
# CONVERT TO INTEGER
# =========================================================

med_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = med_df[
    
    'ScreenTime_Num'
].astype(int)

med_df['Sleep_Num'] = med_df[
    
    'Sleep_Num'
].astype(int)

med_df['CMD_Num'] = med_df[
    
    'CMD_Num'
].astype(int)


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY DATA TYPES
# =========================================================

print("\nMEDIATION DATA TYPES")

print(

    med_df[[

        'ScreenTime_Num',

        'Sleep_Num',

        'CMD_Num'
    ]].dtypes
)


# =========================================================
# PATH A
# SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE
# =========================================================

X_a = sm.add_constant(

    med_df['ScreenTime_Num']
)

model_a = sm.Logit(

    med_df['Sleep_Num'],

    X_a
).fit(disp=False)


print("\n===================================")
print("PATH A")
print("SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE")
print("===================================")

print(model_a.summary())


# =========================================================
# PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT
# SCREEN TIME + SLEEP → CMD
# =========================================================

X_b = med_df[[

    'ScreenTime_Num',

    'Sleep_Num'
]]

X_b = sm.add_constant(X_b)


model_b = sm.Logit(

    med_df['CMD_Num'],

    X_b
).fit(disp=False)


print("\n===================================")
print("PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT")
print("SLEEP CHANGE → CMD")
print("SCREEN TIME → CMD")
print("===================================")

print(model_b.summary())


# =========================================================
# EXTRACT RESULTS
# =========================================================

path_a_coef = model_a.params['ScreenTime_Num']

path_a_p = model_a.pvalues['ScreenTime_Num']


path_b_coef = model_b.params['Sleep_Num']

path_b_p = model_b.pvalues['Sleep_Num']


direct_coef = model_b.params['ScreenTime_Num']

direct_p = model_b.pvalues['ScreenTime_Num']


# =========================================================
# CREATE SUMMARY TABLE
# =========================================================

mediation_summary = pd.DataFrame({

    'Path': [

        'ScreenTime → Sleep',

        'Sleep → CMD',

        'ScreenTime → CMD (Direct Effect)'
    ],

    'Coefficient': [

        path_a_coef,

        path_b_coef,

        direct_coef
    ],

    'P_value': [

        path_a_p,

        path_b_p,

        direct_p
    ]
})


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY SUMMARY TABLE
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("MEDIATION SUMMARY")
print("===================================")

print(

    mediation_summary.round(4)
)


# =========================================================
# EXPORT SUMMARY TABLE
# =========================================================

mediation_summary.to_csv(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Mediation_Analysis_Summary.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)


# =========================================================
# EXPORT FULL MODEL OUTPUTS
# =========================================================

with open(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Mediation_Analysis_Full.txt'
    ),

    'w',

    encoding='utf-8'
) as f:

    f.write(

        "===================================\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "PATH A: SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "===================================\n\n"
    )

    f.write(

        model_a.summary().as_text()
    )

    f.write(

        "\n\n===================================\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "SLEEP CHANGE → CMD\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "SCREEN TIME → CMD\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "===================================\n\n"
    )

    f.write(

        model_b.summary().as_text()
    )


print("\nMediation analysis exported successfully.")
##=====================New====================##
# =========================================================
# INTERACTION ANALYSIS
# SCREEN TIME × SLEEP CHANGE
# =========================================================

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

print("\nRUNNING INTERACTION ANALYSIS")


# =========================================================
# CREATE DATASET
# =========================================================

interaction_df = df[[

    'Average_DD_Use_Time',

    'Sleep_Pattern_Change',

    'CMD'
]].copy()


# =========================================================
# REMOVE MISSING
# =========================================================

interaction_df = interaction_df.dropna()


# =========================================================
# SCREEN TIME NUMERIC
# =========================================================

screen_mapping = {

    '<2': 1,

    '2-4': 2,

    '4-6': 3,

    '>6': 4
}

interaction_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = interaction_df[
    
    'Average_DD_Use_Time'
].map(screen_mapping)


# =========================================================
# CLEAN SLEEP VARIABLE
# =========================================================

interaction_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change'] = (

    interaction_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change']
    .astype(str)
    .str.strip()
    .str.lower()
)


# =========================================================
# SLEEP NUMERIC
# =========================================================

interaction_df['Sleep_Num'] = np.where(

    interaction_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change'].isin([

        'no',
        'no change',
        'normal'
    ]),

    0,

    1
)


# =========================================================
# CMD NUMERIC
# =========================================================

interaction_df['CMD_Num'] = np.where(

    interaction_df['CMD'] == 'CMD Present',

    1,

    0
)


# =========================================================
# INTERACTION TERM
# =========================================================
# =========================================================
# FORCE NUMERIC TYPES
# =========================================================

interaction_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    interaction_df['ScreenTime_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
).astype(int)


interaction_df['Sleep_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    interaction_df['Sleep_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
).astype(int)


# =========================================================
# INTERACTION TERM
# =========================================================

interaction_df['Interaction'] = (

    interaction_df['ScreenTime_Num'] *

    interaction_df['Sleep_Num']
)

# =========================================================
# FINAL DATA
# =========================================================

interaction_df = interaction_df[[

    'ScreenTime_Num',

    'Sleep_Num',

    'Interaction',

    'CMD_Num'
]].dropna()


# =========================================================
# X AND y
# =========================================================

X_interaction = interaction_df[[

    'ScreenTime_Num',

    'Sleep_Num',

    'Interaction'
]]

y_interaction = interaction_df['CMD_Num']


# =========================================================
# FIT MODEL
# =========================================================

interaction_model = LogisticRegression(

    max_iter=5000
)

interaction_model.fit(

    X_interaction,

    y_interaction
)


# =========================================================
# COEFFICIENTS
# =========================================================

coef_df = pd.DataFrame({

    'Variable': X_interaction.columns,

    'Coefficient': interaction_model.coef_[0]
})


print("\n===================================")
print("INTERACTION ANALYSIS RESULTS")
print("===================================")

print(coef_df)


# =========================================================
# EXPORT RESULTS
# =========================================================

coef_df.to_csv(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Interaction_Analysis.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)


print("\nInteraction analysis exported successfully.")'''













































# =========================================================
# 34. CMD PREVALENCE FIGURE
# =========================================================

cmd_prev = df['CMD'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100

colors = ['skyblue', 'salmon']

ax = cmd_prev.plot(
    kind='bar',
    color=colors,
    figsize=(6, 6),
    width=0.6
)

# Publication style
ax.grid(False)
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)

plt.xlabel(
    'CMD Status',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.ylabel(
    'Percentage (%)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.title(
    'CMD Prevalence',
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight='bold'
)

for index, value in enumerate(cmd_prev):

    ax.text(
        index,
        value + 1,
        f'{value:.1f}%',
        ha='center',
        fontsize=11,
        fontweight='bold'
    )

plt.xticks(rotation=0, fontsize=11)
plt.yticks(fontsize=11)

plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'CMD_Prevalence.png'
    ),
    dpi=300,
    bbox_inches='tight'
)

plt.show()






# =========================================================
# CMD TREND VISUALIZATION
# DIGITAL DEVICE USE vs CMD
# PUBLICATION-QUALITY FIGURE
# =========================================================

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os


# =========================================================
# 1. CREATE TREND TABLE
# =========================================================

trend_table = pd.crosstab(

    df['Average_DD_Use_Time'],
    df['CMD'],

    normalize='index'
) * 100


# =========================================================
# 2. REORDER CATEGORIES
# =========================================================

desired_order = [

    '<2',
    '2-4',
    '4-6',
    '>6'
]

trend_table = trend_table.reindex(desired_order)


# =========================================================
# 3. DISPLAY TABLE
# =========================================================

print("\nCMD TREND TABLE (%)")

print(trend_table)


# =========================================================
# 4. DEFINE OUTPUT DIRECTORY
# =========================================================

OUTPUT_DIR = r'G:\Documents\Book\1\4th Year\4th\1822021 (Project)\Trial\Final_Output\Combining_All'

os.makedirs(

    OUTPUT_DIR,

    exist_ok=True
)

# 5. FORMAT TABLE FOR EXCEL EXPORT
# =========================================================

trend_table_export = trend_table.copy()

trend_table_export.index = [
    'Less than 2 h',
    '2 to 4 h',
    '4 to 6 h',
    'More than 6 h'
]

# =========================================================
# 6. EXPORT TABLE
# =========================================================

trend_table_export.to_csv(
    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'CMD_Trend_Table.csv'
    ),
    encoding='utf-8-sig'
)

print("\nTrend table exported successfully.")





######=======New====================####
# =========================================================
# COCHRAN-ARMITAGE TREND TEST
# =========================================================

from statsmodels.stats.contingency_tables import Table


# =========================================================
# CREATE COUNT TABLE
# =========================================================

trend_counts = pd.crosstab(

    df['Average_DD_Use_Time'],
    df['CMD']
)


# =========================================================
# REORDER GROUPS
# =========================================================

trend_counts = trend_counts.reindex(

    ['<2', '2-4', '4-6', '>6']
)


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY TABLE
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("COCHRAN-ARMITAGE TREND TEST TABLE")
print("===================================")

print(trend_counts)


# =========================================================
# PERFORM TREND TEST
# =========================================================

table = Table(

    trend_counts.values
)

trend_result = table.test_ordinal_association()


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY RESULTS
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("COCHRAN-ARMITAGE TREND TEST")
print("===================================")

print(

    f"Z-statistic : {trend_result.zscore:.4f}"
)

print(

    f"P-value     : {trend_result.pvalue:.6f}"
)


# =========================================================
# SAVE RESULTS
# =========================================================

trend_test_df = pd.DataFrame({

    'Statistic': [

        'Z_statistic',
        'P_value'
    ],

    'Value': [

        trend_result.zscore,
        trend_result.pvalue
    ]
})


trend_test_df.to_csv(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Cochran_Armitage_Trend_Test.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)


print("\nTrend test results exported successfully.")









"""# =========================================================
# SUPPLEMENTARY ANALYSIS
# MEDIATION ANALYSIS
# SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE → CMD
# =========================================================

# NOTE:
#
# - Exploratory supplementary analysis
# - NOT causal inference
# - Simplified linear mediation approach
# - Used due to instability of logistic mediation
#
# =========================================================

import statsmodels.api as sm

print("\nRUNNING MEDIATION ANALYSIS")


# =========================================================
# CREATE DATASET
# =========================================================

med_df = df[[

    'Average_DD_Use_Time',

    'Sleep_Pattern_Change',

    'CMD'
]].copy()


# =========================================================
# REMOVE MISSING VALUES
# =========================================================

med_df = med_df.dropna()


# =========================================================
# SCREEN TIME NUMERIC
# =========================================================

screen_mapping = {

    '<2': 1,

    '2-4': 2,

    '4-6': 3,

    '>6': 4
}

med_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = med_df[
    
    'Average_DD_Use_Time'
].map(screen_mapping)


# =========================================================
# SLEEP CHANGE NUMERIC
# =========================================================

# 0 = No sleep change
# 1 = Sleep change present

med_df['Sleep_Num'] = np.where(

    med_df['Sleep_Pattern_Change'] == 'No',

    0,

    1
)


# =========================================================
# CMD NUMERIC
# =========================================================

med_df['CMD_Num'] = np.where(

    med_df['CMD'] == 'CMD Present',

    1,

    0
)


# =========================================================
# FORCE NUMERIC
# =========================================================

med_df['ScreenTime_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['ScreenTime_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['Sleep_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['Sleep_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)

med_df['CMD_Num'] = pd.to_numeric(

    med_df['CMD_Num'],

    errors='coerce'
)


# =========================================================
# REMOVE NA
# =========================================================

med_df = med_df.dropna()


# =========================================================
# PATH A
# SCREEN TIME → SLEEP CHANGE
# =========================================================

X_a = sm.add_constant(

    med_df['ScreenTime_Num']
)

y_a = med_df['Sleep_Num']


model_a = sm.OLS(

    y_a,

    X_a
).fit()


# =========================================================
# PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT
# SCREEN TIME + SLEEP → CMD
# =========================================================

X_b = med_df[[

    'ScreenTime_Num',

    'Sleep_Num'
]]

X_b = sm.add_constant(X_b)

y_b = med_df['CMD_Num']


model_b = sm.OLS(

    y_b,

    X_b
).fit()


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY RESULTS
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("PATH A")
print("SCREEN TIME -> SLEEP CHANGE")
print("===================================")

print(model_a.summary())


print("\n===================================")
print("PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT")
print("SLEEP CHANGE -> CMD")
print("SCREEN TIME -> CMD")
print("===================================")

print(model_b.summary())


# =========================================================
# EXTRACT COEFFICIENTS
# =========================================================

path_a_coef = model_a.params['ScreenTime_Num']

path_a_p = model_a.pvalues['ScreenTime_Num']


path_b_coef = model_b.params['Sleep_Num']

path_b_p = model_b.pvalues['Sleep_Num']


direct_coef = model_b.params['ScreenTime_Num']

direct_p = model_b.pvalues['ScreenTime_Num']


# =========================================================
# INDIRECT EFFECT
# =========================================================

indirect_effect = path_a_coef * path_b_coef


# =========================================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
# =========================================================

mediation_summary = pd.DataFrame({

    'Path': [

        'ScreenTime -> Sleep',

        'Sleep -> CMD',

        'ScreenTime -> CMD (Direct)',

        'Indirect Effect'
    ],

    'Coefficient': [

        round(path_a_coef, 4),

        round(path_b_coef, 4),

        round(direct_coef, 4),

        round(indirect_effect, 4)
    ],

    'P_value': [

        round(path_a_p, 4),

        round(path_b_p, 4),

        round(direct_p, 4),

        np.nan
    ]
})


# =========================================================
# DISPLAY SUMMARY
# =========================================================

print("\n===================================")
print("MEDIATION SUMMARY")
print("===================================")

print(mediation_summary)


# =========================================================
# EXPORT RESULTS
# =========================================================

mediation_summary.to_csv(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Supplementary_Mediation_Analysis.csv'
    ),

    index=False
)


# =========================================================
# SAVE FULL MODEL OUTPUTS
# =========================================================

with open(

    os.path.join(

        OUTPUT_DIR,

        'Supplementary_Mediation_Full_Results.txt'
    ),

    'w',

    encoding='utf-8'
) as f:

    f.write(

        "===================================\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "PATH A: SCREEN TIME -> SLEEP CHANGE\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "===================================\n\n"
    )

    f.write(

        model_a.summary().as_text()
    )

    f.write(

        "\n\n===================================\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "PATH B + DIRECT EFFECT\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "SLEEP CHANGE -> CMD\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "SCREEN TIME -> CMD\n"
    )

    f.write(

        "===================================\n\n"
    )

    f.write(

        model_b.summary().as_text()
    )


print("\nMEDIATION ANALYSIS COMPLETED")"""










# =========================================================
# 6. EXTRACT VALUES
# =========================================================

# SAFE COLUMN EXTRACTION

if 'CMD Present' in trend_table.columns:

    cmd_present = trend_table['CMD Present'].values

else:

    cmd_present = trend_table.iloc[:,0].values


if 'No CMD' in trend_table.columns:

    no_cmd = trend_table['No CMD'].values

else:

    no_cmd = trend_table.iloc[:,1].values


# =========================================================
# 7. CREATE FIGURE
# =========================================================

fig, ax = plt.subplots(
    figsize=(10,7)
)

# Publication style
ax.grid(False)
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)


# =========================================================
# 8. CMD PRESENT LINE
# =========================================================

ax.plot(

    desired_order,
    cmd_present,

    marker='o',

    linewidth=3,

    markersize=10,

    label='CMD Present'
)


# =========================================================
# 9. NO CMD LINE
# =========================================================

ax.plot(

    desired_order,
    no_cmd,

    marker='o',

    linewidth=3,

    markersize=10,

    label='No CMD'
)


# =========================================================
# 10. ADD LABELS TO CMD LINE
# =========================================================

for i, value in enumerate(cmd_present):

    ax.text(

        i,

        value + 2,

        f'{value:.1f}%',

        ha='center',

        fontsize=11,

        fontweight='bold'
    )


# =========================================================
# 11. LABELS
# =========================================================

ax.set_xlabel(
    'Average Daily Digital Device Use Time (Hours)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

ax.set_ylabel(
    'Percentage (%)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

ax.set_title(

    'Trend of CMD Across Digital Device Use Time',

    fontsize=14,

    fontweight='bold'
)


# =========================================================
# 12. Y LIMIT
# =========================================================

ax.set_ylim(0, 100)




# =========================================================
# 13. LEGEND
# =========================================================

ax.legend()

# =========================================================
ax.tick_params(
    axis='both',
    labelsize=11
)

ax.legend(
    fontsize=11,
    frameon=False
)


# =========================================================
# 15. SAVE FIGURE
# =========================================================

plt.tight_layout()

figure_path = os.path.join(

    OUTPUT_DIR,

    'CMD_Trend_Figure.png'
)

plt.savefig(

    figure_path,

    dpi=300,

    bbox_inches='tight'
)

print("\nCMD trend figure exported successfully.")

print("\nSaved Location:")

print(figure_path)


# =========================================================
# 16. SHOW FIGURE
# =========================================================

plt.show()





# =========================================================
# 35. CMD VS SCREEN TIME
# =========================================================

cmd_table = pd.crosstab(

    df['Average_DD_Use_Time'],
    df['CMD'],

    normalize='index'
) * 100

order = ['<2', '2-4', '4-6', '>6']

cmd_table = cmd_table.reindex(order)

ax = cmd_table.plot(
    kind='bar',
    stacked=False,
    figsize=(10,7),
    width=0.75
)

# Publication style
ax.grid(False)
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)


plt.xlabel(
    'Average Daily Digital Device Use Time (Hours)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.ylabel(
    'Percentage (%)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.title(
    'CMD vs Digital Device Use Time',
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight='bold'
)

for container in ax.containers:

    for bar in container:

        height = bar.get_height()

        ax.text(

            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,

            height + 1,

            f'{height:.1f}%',

            ha='center',

            va='bottom',

            rotation=65,

            fontsize=9,

            fontweight='bold'
        )


plt.xticks(rotation=0, fontsize=11)
plt.yticks(fontsize=11)


plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'CMD_vs_Screen_Time.png'
    ),

    dpi=300,
    bbox_inches='tight'
)

plt.show()


# =========================================================
# 36. CMD SEVERITY ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

severity_table = pd.crosstab(

    df['Average_DD_Use_Time'],
    df['CMD_Severity'],

    normalize='index'
) * 100

severity_table = severity_table.reindex(order)

ax = severity_table.plot(
    kind='bar',
    stacked=False,
    figsize=(12,8),
    width=0.75
)

# Publication style
ax.grid(False)
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)

plt.xlabel(
    'Average Daily Digital Device Use Time (Hours)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.ylabel(
    'Percentage (%)',
    fontsize=12,
    fontweight='bold'
)

plt.title(
    'CMD Severity vs Digital Device Use Time',
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight='bold'
)

for container in ax.containers:

    for bar in container:

        height = bar.get_height()

        ax.text(

            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,

            height + 1,

            f'{height:.1f}%',

            ha='center',

            va='bottom',

            rotation=65,

            fontsize=9,

            fontweight='bold'
        )



plt.title('CMD Severity vs Digital Device Use Time')

plt.xticks(rotation=0, fontsize=11)
plt.yticks(fontsize=11)


plt.tight_layout()

plt.savefig(

    os.path.join(
        OUTPUT_DIR,
        'CMD_Severity_vs_Screen_Time.png'
    ),

    dpi=300,
    bbox_inches='tight'
)

plt.show()


# =========================================================
# 37. FINAL STATUS
# =========================================================

print("""

===================================
FINAL ADVANCED CMD ANALYSIS COMPLETE
===================================

GENERATED:

1. Multi-model machine learning evaluation
2. Logistic Regression, KNN, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, AdaBoost, CatBoost, and Stacking models
3. Confusion matrix analysis
4. ROC curve comparison
5. AUROC performance evaluation
6. Cross-validation analysis
7. XGBoost feature importance analysis
8. SHAP explainability analysis
9. SHAP dependence plots
10. Logistic regression adjusted odds ratios (AOR)
11. CMD prevalence analysis
12. CMD vs digital device use trend analysis
13. CMD severity vs digital device use analysis
14. Cochran-Armitage trend test
15. Mediation analysis  X
16. Interaction analysis
17. Publication-quality figures
18. Calibration-ready prediction framework
19. Excel, CSV, TXT, and figure exports
20. Advanced explainable AI workflow
21. Ensemble learning framework
22. Digital device use behavioral risk modeling
23. Publication-ready statistical reporting
""")
